Fixed by giving `#qc-scheme-root` `height:100vh` and making `.app` `flex:1; min-height:0`. Added `overflow:hidden` to `.sidebar` so `.tree` (`flex:1; overflow-y:auto`) scrolls correctly. Removed `max-height: calc(100vh - 110px)` workaround from `.sidebar`.

---

### 2026-04-24 — codebook.json full schema normalization

All codes in `codebook.json` now have a complete record with fields in canonical order: `name`, `parent`, `scope`, `rationale`, `usage_notes`, `provenance`, `status`, `pinned_examples`, `_log`. Previously only 56 documented codes had records; all 1157 codes now have full records with empty defaults for undocumented fields. Migration runs automatically in `_docs_load` and `_docs_load_json` on first load, reading parent values from the sibling `codebook.yaml`. `examples` field migrated to `pinned_examples`. `_baseline` stripped on load.

---

### 2026-04-24 — Snapshot UI in qc-refactor

Snapshot management added to qc-refactor as a top-level mode toggle in the mode bar (Refactor | Snapshots), matching qc-scheme's appMode pattern. Snapshots mode replaces the full app view with a dedicated panel.

**Create zone:** label input with live filename preview, description textarea, Save button. Filename generated as `codebook_YYYYMMDD-HHMM_label`.

**Snapshot feed:** existing snapshots listed newest-first with timestamp, label, dirname, note, and Open button. Opening a snapshot calls `POST /snapshots/load` which copies the snapshot's `codebook.yaml` and `codebook.json` over the working files, then refreshes the runtime tree and docs without re-render.

New server endpoint `POST /snapshots/load` added.

---

### 2026-04-24 — @ code autocomplete in text inputs

New shared JavaScript module `qc-shared.js` added to `qc-atelier/shared/`. Contains `qcAutocompleteInit(codeNames)`, a self-contained autocomplete system that attaches to all `<input type="text">` and `<textarea>` elements on the page via event delegation.

**Behaviour:** typing `@` anywhere in a text field opens a floating dropdown of matching code names. The query is everything typed after `@` until a whitespace or delimiter character. Matches are sorted prefix-first, then substring. The matched portion is highlighted in the dropdown. Arrow keys navigate, Enter or Tab selects, Escape dismisses. On selection, the `@query` is replaced in-place with the code name.

**Integration:** both `qc-scheme-filter.lua` and `qc-refactor-filter.lua` updated to read and inline `qc-shared.js` before the tool JS. `qcAutocompleteInit` is called with the current `treeArr` code names after `refreshTreeFromServer()` in qc-scheme and after `refreshTree()` in qc-refactor, keeping the dropdown list current after structural changes without re-render.

Autocomplete dropdown CSS added to `qc-shared.css`.

This establishes `qc-shared.js` as the home for shared runtime JavaScript across the suite, parallel to `qc-shared.lua` (render-time) and `qc-shared.css` (styles).

---

### 2026-04-24 — qc-reflect renamed to qc-align; qc-unfold defined

**qc-reflect renamed to qc-align.** The original name did not clearly communicate the tool's function. The reframing clarified a fundamental distinction in the suite: qc-align operates on the *code system*, using the corpus as evidence; a separate tool (qc-unfold) operates on the *corpus text*, using the code system as an analytical lens. This axis — instrument calibration vs. instrument application — maps onto how qualitative research actually works and gives each tool a clear, non-overlapping mandate.

**qc-unfold defined.** The synthesis and meaning-making functions originally sketched for qc-reflect (biographies, summaries, figuration detection) belong in a separate tool because they represent a later analytical phase. You would not derive meaning from the corpus using an inconsistent or bloated code system; qc-unfold is downstream of qc-align.

**Naming process.** Extensive naming discussion led to: `qc-align` (code system maintenance — bringing the code system into alignment with itself and the corpus) and `qc-unfold` (corpus meaning-making — unfolding what the coded corpus contains). Both names may be revisited.

---

### 2026-04-24 — qc-align technical specification

**Scope.** Six audit modes: Overlap, Inconsistency, Bloat, Restructure, Line splitting, Code propagation. Additional modes deferred to future iterations.

**Sparse and Undocumented modes excluded.** Sparse codes are visible in qc-scheme via use counts in the sidebar — not an alignment problem. Undocumented codes are a qc-scheme workflow issue. Neither requires LLM analysis.

**Hierarchy context in prompts.** All prompts include parent and grandparent documentation (scope + rationale) as a framing block. Rationale: a flat list of code names gives the LLM insufficient context to reason about analytical boundaries. The parent's documented scope defines what the category is supposed to carve out, which grounds assessment of whether children are doing their job and whether siblings are genuinely distinct.

**Candidate selection is user-driven.** Auto-detection heuristics (co-occurrence threshold, minimum sibling count) were considered and rejected as arbitrary. The user selects the scope via a multi-select tree picker, which drives candidate construction for any mode.

**Line splitting and code propagation are separate modes.** Both involve sequential reading of the corpus but touch different things: line splitting modifies corpus files (via `qc corpus update`); code propagation modifies the database (via SQLite batch write). Treating them as the same operation would blur the distinction between corpus integrity and coding completeness.

**Line splitting is full-corpus, on-demand.** Scanning only lines with N+ codes was considered and rejected as arbitrary. The LLM determines whether a line is topically composite; the user triggers the scan explicitly when ready.

**Speaker prefix constraint.** All corpus lines have a `Speaker: text` format. Line splitting always preserves the speaker prefix on each resulting line. Merging lines across speakers is never permitted. Code propagation may propose codes across speaker boundaries based on conversational context, but never merges transcript lines.

**Backup and snapshot policy.** Every destructive operation (database write, corpus file edit, structural change) creates both a timestamped SQLite backup and a codebook snapshot before execution. Rationale: qc-align can substantially reshape the code system across multiple audit sessions; restore points must exist at every intervention, not just at named milestones.

**Dryrun mechanism.** All destructive operations show an exact preview before execution. Rationale: the consequences of bulk code propagation or line splitting are significant and not easily reversible without the backup mechanism; users must see exactly what will change before confirming.

**Model assignment.** Reasoning-intensive modes (Overlap, Inconsistency, Bloat, Restructure) use `qwen3:35b`. Generative modes (Line splitting, Code propagation) use `qwen3:8b`. Rationale: the reasoning modes require careful analytical judgment about code boundaries and relationships; the generative modes are primarily reading and segmentation tasks where a smaller model performs adequately at significantly lower latency.

**Refactor integration.** Accepted structural suggestions (Overlap, Bloat, Restructure) pre-populate the qc-refactor queue via `POST /refactor/queue`. No direct execution from qc-align. Rationale: structural changes to the code system should be reviewed in qc-refactor before execution, maintaining the principle that qc-refactor is the single point of structural execution.

**Code propagation and direct SQLite writes.** This is the only place in qc-atelier where the database is written to directly rather than through the qc CLI. There is no `qc codes apply` CLI command. Mitigated by mandatory backup, dryrun preview, and session logging. This should be revisited if a programmatic apply command is added to qc upstream.

---

### 2026-04-24 — qc-align foundation; queue conflict validation; launcher update

**qc-reflect renamed to qc-align.** All files, filter globals (`REFLECT_CONFIG` → `ALIGN_CONFIG`), root element IDs, and config sections renamed. The existing co-occurrence/overlap/chat machinery is retained as the foundation for the Overlap audit mode.

**qc-align-filter.lua.** Rebuilt from qc-reflect-filter.lua applying current shared infrastructure standards: `qc-shared.lua` loaded via `dofile()` with `_get_project_root_early()` path resolution; shared CSS, shared JS, and nav bar injected; `ALIGN_CONFIG` global extended with `server_port`, `scheme_path`, `json_dir`, and per-mode model assignments. Old duplicated helper functions removed.

**qc-align.js — audit palette and suggestion system.** Six audit modes defined: Overlap, Inconsistency, Bloat, Restructure, Line splitting, Code propagation. Left panel replaced with audit palette (mode selector buttons), scope picker (multi-select collapsible tree, same pattern as qc-refactor Move form), Run button, and session summary. Suggestion card system added with accept/reject/defer actions, decision note input, and status display. Accepted suggestions with a `refactor_op` field are sent to `/refactor/queue`. All decisions logged to `/align/log`.

**Prompt strategy implemented for Overlap and Bloat.** Both prompts include a hierarchy context block (ancestry paths with scope + rationale at each level). Overlap prompt presents both codes with documentation, excerpts, and co-occurrence count. Bloat prompt presents the parent's documentation alongside all sibling codes. Both request structured JSON responses parsed by `ollamaJSON()`.

**Queue conflict validation in qc-refactor.** `validateQueueConflicts()` runs before any operation is added to the queue. Detects: source code already renamed in queue, source code already merged away, target code already transformed, deprecated code being structurally modified, duplicate operation. Conflict shown as inline form error; staging blocked.

**Server endpoints added:** `GET /align/log` and `POST /align/log` (append-only session log at `qc/align-log.json`); `POST /refactor/queue` (stores ops in `qc/refactor-queue.json` for qc-refactor to pick up on next load).

**Refactor queue pre-population.** On init, qc-refactor reads `qc/refactor-queue.json` and loads any pending ops into the queue, then clears the file. This is the handoff mechanism from qc-align accepted suggestions to qc-refactor execution.

**Launcher updated.** qc-align card added as active (links to `/qc-align.html`). qc-unfold added as inactive stub. qc-reflect card removed.

**Remaining for qc-align:** Inconsistency, Restructure, Line splitting, and Code propagation audit modes; right panel suggestion display; dryrun + backup mechanism; `qc-align.qmd` creation.

---

### 2026-04-29 — qc-align: focused coding reframing, testing infrastructure, UI overhaul

**Fundamental reframing.** The original overlap prompt asked "should these codes be merged?" — wrong framing for constructivist grounded theory. Co-occurrence is expected and correct in qualitative coding; multiple codes on the same passage capture different analytical dimensions. The correct question, following Charmaz and Saldaña, is: does each code have distinct analytical weight that will contribute to theory-building in focused coding? The prompt and suggestion types were rewritten accordingly.

**Audit mode consolidation.** Six modes (Overlap, Inconsistency, Bloat, Restructure, Line splitting, Code propagation) consolidated to four: Focus (replaces Overlap + Bloat), Consistency, Restructure, Corpus (Line splitting + Code propagation). Sparse and Undocumented were already excluded. Focus and Bloat were asking the same question at different scopes — whether codes are doing distinct analytical work — and were merged. Consistency, Restructure, and Corpus remain stubs pending implementation.

**Two-turn LLM strategy.** Sending full docs for all 934 codes in one prompt exceeded the context window on 32GB unified memory. The two-turn approach: Turn 1 sends a compact tree outline (names + parents only, ~5k tokens) + focal pair docs + excerpts, asks the LLM to identify which other codes are relevant. Turn 2 sends focal pairs + docs for identified codes only (~10-15k tokens), asks for the actual assessment. This gives the LLM genuine system-wide discovery capability without overloading the context window.

**Model configuration.** `qwen3.5:35b` confirmed as the correct model (was incorrectly set to `qwen3:35b`). `num_ctx` set to 49152 — comfortable for two-turn prompts on 32GB. `qwen2.5:7b` used for fast testing.

**Testing infrastructure.** Four controls added below the Run button:
- Mock mode — bypasses LLM entirely, returns randomised fake suggestions for UI testing
- Prompt preview — builds and displays Turn 1 prompt without sending to LLM
- Fast mode — uses `qwen2.5:7b`, caps at 3 pairs
- Load response — loads a previously saved response file

Successful runs auto-save to `qc/align-responses/align_mode_TIMESTAMP.json`. Server endpoints `GET /align/responses` and `POST /align/responses/save` added.

**Suggestion types.** Removed `merge` and `rename` from Focus mode (belong in qc-refactor after deliberate researcher decision). New types: `restructure-subcode`, `separate-dimension`, `candidate-category`, `document-distinction`, `review-application`.

**Report design decision.** Current card-per-suggestion design rejected — treats suggestions as independent when the audit is holistic. Planned redesign: one report per code pair, structured as: (1) comparison table with LLM-synthesised analytical dimensions, (2) arguments for distinguishing, (3) arguments for restructuring, (4) coding inconsistencies with specific lines to recode, (5) bottom line. Actions (recode, rename, move, merge, document) queued internally within qc-align, not sent directly to qc-refactor.

**UI fixes.** Title corrected to QC Align. LLM badge reflects actual model in use. Light/dark mode toggle added, synced with `qc.scheme.theme` localStorage. Clear + Run combined into single action row. Defer button no longer greyed out. Overlap analysis remnant removed from sidebar.

**Bug fix.** Orphaned `runBloatAudit` fragment (escaped from function body during refactor) caused `ReferenceError: codes is not defined` on boot. Removed. Multiple unguarded accesses to `report.codes`, `report.suggestions`, `report.chat` also fixed.

---

# 2026-04-30 — qc-reflect rebuild, qc-refactor fixes, GPU cluster

## qc-reflect (complete rebuild from scratch)

Previous qc-align work discarded entirely. qc-reflect rebuilt as a focused analytical tool for code system refinement per Charmaz/Saldaña focused coding methods.

**Purpose:** Give the researcher feedback on their own coding practice — where application diverges from intent, where patterns are inconsistent, where the code system could be more comprehensive. Not about reducing codes but clarifying analytical distinctions.

**Architecture:**
- `qc-reflect-filter.lua` — bakes CODEBOOK_TREE, CORPUS_INDEX, COOC_DATA, ALL_CODES, DOC_NAMES, REFLECT_CONFIG into HTML
- `qc-reflect.js` — full JS shell (tree picker, run, auto-detect, prior runs, report renderer)
- `qc-reflect.css` — styles
- Server: `POST /reflect/run` (saves JSON + QMD, calls quarto render, returns HTML path), `GET /reflect/reports`

**UI structure:**
- Left sidebar: full code tree for scope selection, auto-detect button, prior runs list (scrollable)
- Right panel: report for current run, Generate table button, Open rendered button
- Topbar: Mock / Prompt testing controls, model badge

**Report structure (structured JSON, not prose):**
```json
{
  "per_code": [{ "name", "summary", "misapplications": [{"doc","line","text","explanation"}] }],
  "cross_code": {
    "redundancies": [{"codes","explanation","passages"}],
    "tensions": [{"codes","explanation","passages"}],
    "new_codes": [{"name","rationale","scope","passages"}]
  },
  "bottom_line": { "assessment", "recommendations": [] }
}
```

**Two LLM calls:**
1. Auto-detect: compact tree + selected code docs → list of related codes to add to selection
2. Report: full code docs + excerpts → structured JSON report

**QMD output:** Proper Quarto document with callout blocks for misapplications (warning), new code suggestions (tip), tabset per code, collapsed methods note, synthesis table on demand. Rendered via `quarto render` on server, opened in new tab.

**Citation format:** `[DocName:LineNum]` e.g. `[BMazer:125]`

**Auto-detection signals:** co-occurrence, similar names, similar documentation, same branch, cross-branch occurrence.

**Testing controls:** Mock mode (fake structured JSON), Prompt preview (show prompt without running), model badge.

## qc-refactor fixes

**Snapshots panel missing:** Filter had no `#snapshots-view`, `#snapshots-panel`, or `.qr-mode-btn` elements. Added Refactor/Snapshots toggle topbar and snapshots view to filter HTML.

**Changes not reflected in qc-scheme:** After execute, codebook.yaml is updated but qc-scheme.html is a static baked file. Added "Re-render qc-scheme" button to results panel — triggers `POST /refactor/rerender` which calls `quarto render qc-scheme.qmd` on server.

**Deprecate multi-select:** Replaced single-code input with same collapsible tree picker as Move. Each selected code gets its own queue item.

**Server:** Added `/refactor/rerender` endpoint.

## qc-scheme fixes

**Return to HEAD:** Added always-visible context pill to topbar showing current state. When at HEAD: muted "HEAD" label. When snapshot open: snapshot name + "↩ HEAD" button that resets `openedSnapshotDir` and `openedDocsPath` and reloads tree from `/refactor/tree`.

**Bug:** IIFE result not passed as h() argument (missing comma). Fixed.

## GPU cluster setup

Cluster: `132.216.183.78`, GPU 5 on port 11435, `ollama_gpu_5` container.
SSH tunnel: `ssh -fN -L 11444:localhost:11435 zack@132.216.183.78`
Config: `ollama.url: http://localhost:11444`, `model: qwen2.5:7b`, `num_ctx: 32768`

Issue: `--restart always` causes container to restart without GPU binding after cluster reboot. Fix: relaunch manually with `docker rm -f ollama_gpu_5 && docker run -d --gpus "device=5" ...`. Script saved as `~/start_ollama.sh` on cluster.

qwen3.5:35b available for production runs (23GB, fits in 24GB VRAM). Switch via config when ready.

---

### 2026-05-06 — qc-reflect: Quarto report overhaul, suggestion engine, stub operations, branch colour coding

**Quarto report structure.** Rebuilt from scratch with a funnel structure: per-code tabset first (specific misapplications with remove checkboxes), then cross-code observations (redundancies and tensions), then suggested new codes (with editable scope, passage selection, and add-to-codebook), then bottom line (assessment, recommendations, pending changes summary, execute button). The execute section lists all queued changes and shows success/failure feedback after running, including removed count, added count, new codes, prerender status, and backup path.

**OJS interactivity fixes.** Checkboxes rebuilt using the `viewof` reactive pattern — the original single-cell approach with inline `addEventListener` was causing double-add on re-render. Mutable-only OJS cells now return `html\`<span></span>\`` to suppress the `undefined` output that was appearing below every checkbox. Passage text uses DOM `createTextNode` and `appendChild` rather than single-quoted `innerHTML` strings to avoid apostrophe and quote escaping breaking OJS parsing. Code names marked up as chips with branch colours throughout the report. `buildQMD()` updated to match all of the above.

**Suggest-driven launcher.** Inline report rendering removed — the Quarto HTML is the real output. The in-browser interface is now a launcher: select a grouping, inspect it, run the deep LLM analysis, open the report. Five suggestion methods run entirely in-browser on baked data: co-occurrence clustering (Jaccard similarity, top-quartile cutoff shown explicitly), tree proximity (sibling branch coverage), undercoded (z-score vs sibling mean, threshold z < −1 and sibling mean > 2), high-variance (CV > 1.0 across documents, grouped by branch proximity), LLM semantic (separate opt-in call using co-occurrence data and codebook structure). Groupings named and described by a small model via `naming_model` config key.

**Visualisations.** Detail pane shows a D3 force/radial/grid network of the selected grouping plus 1-hop co-occurrence neighbours, with zoom, pan, and drag. Layout toggleable between force simulation, radial (suggestion codes in inner ring), and fixed grid. Method-specific charts: Jaccard heatmap matrix (co-occurrence), deviation bar chart with sibling mean line (undercoded/tree-proximity), per-document sparklines with area fill (high-variance), horizontal bar chart (other). Corpus overview treemap at rest, sized by use count, coloured by branch, with top co-occurring pairs listed below.

**Filter updates.** Exports `DOC_CODE_MATRIX` (document → code list, needed for variance and undercoded methods) and `naming_model`. D3 7.9.0 loaded via cdnjs CDN.

**Stub convention.** Stubs defined as codes matching `^\d{2}_[A-Za-z][A-Za-z_]*$` — two-digit prefix, word-only label, no numeric suffix segment. Marked `status: stub` in `codebook.json`. Create stub tab added to qc-refactor: name field and optional parent picker. Server-side `_do_create_stub()` inserts into `codebook.yaml` with correct indentation and updates `codebook.json`. No SQLite or corpus changes needed — stubs are never applied to coded lines. Stubs excluded from reflect suggestion candidates. JD transition strategy agreed: designate current `XX_Label` nodes as stubs, strip numeric middle segments from leaves, then evaluate whether branch prefixes are still needed.

**Branch colour coding.** `isStub()`, `getCodeColor()`, `desaturateHex()`, `codeDot()` added to `qc-shared.js`, available to all tools. All filters export `CODE_COLORS` and `CODE_SCHEMA` from `code_schema.colors` in config, keyed by two-digit prefix. qc-scheme renders stubs bold in their full branch colour; leaf codes rendered in a desaturated version (45%); status pip carries branch colour rather than accent.

**qc-refactor layout.** Split into three columns: op form (left, resizable), staged queue (middle), preview/script/results (right). Doc panels removed from op forms and moved into foldable sections on queue items, toggled via ≡ button, one open at a time. Sidebar widened to 380px; op tab padding reduced to fit all five tabs (Rename, Merge, Move, Deprecate, Create stub) on one row. Move picker list vertically resizable via JS drag handle constrained to viewport bottom.

**Pending.** Stub rendering in qc-reflect suggestion engine (tabled). Colour coding in autocomplete dropdowns. JD prefix stripping workflow in qc-refactor.

---

### 2026-05-06 — project restructure: consolidate qc-atelier tooling

- Move qc-atelier-config.yaml and qc-atelier-server.py from root into qc-atelier/
- Move tool .qmd entry points into their respective tool directories
- Update config path in all four filters and server; QC_ATELIER_CONFIG env var override still works
- Exclude qc-atelier/ from Quarto website render in _quarto.yml — tool .qmds were previously rendered as website pages via *.qmd glob
- Remove stray qualitative_coding.log from project root
- Add .gitignore

---

### 2026-05-06 — nav, index, colour coding, config cleanup

- Add reflect to nav in all four tools; add inactive placeholders for
  qc-align, qc-unfold, qc-trace; reorder viz, scheme, refactor, reflect,
  align, unfold, trace
- Move index.html from qc/ to qc-atelier/; recast as two-column grid;
  reflect promoted to active, align recast as corpus adjustment tool,
  trace added as planned diagnostic tool for codebook history
- build_code_colors() added to qc-shared.lua; colours derived from codebook
  prefixes at render time using a palette; per-branch config entries removed
- Blacklist removed from config; qc-viz now excludes deprecated codes by
  reading codebook.json status at render time
- Rendered HTMLs moved from qc/ to qc-atelier/; server split into SERVE_DIR
  (qc-atelier/) for HTML and DATA_DIR (qc/) for codebook and corpus data

---

### 2026-05-06 — fix filter path resolution, server routing

- All four filters updated to resolve project root by stripping two path
  components from pwd — necessary because quarto cds to the qmd directory
  before running filters, breaking the previous pwd-based fallback
- viz filter updated to prepend project root to CSS, JS, corpus, and
  codebook paths, which were resolving relative to the wrong directory
- Server adds /reflect-reports/ route serving static files from DATA_DIR (qc/)
- Server serves index.html from qc-atelier/ via dedicated root handler

---

### 2026-05-07 — qc-scheme stubs and colour coding; server fixes

STUBS
- ⊕ icon for stubs in tree
- stub added as selectable status in documentation dropdown
- deprecated codes sorted to bottom at every tree level
- documentation indicators removed

COLOUR CODING
- tree nodes: coloured left border, stubs full saturation, leaves
  desaturated; right pane title same treatment
- autocomplete dropdown items show branch colour as left border
- getCodeColor() walks CODEBOOK_TREE for ancestor colour on nested stubs
- codeChip() added to qc-shared.js for use across all tools
- chip font size matches surrounding text (0.9em)

RICH TEXT FIELDS
- makeRichField() wraps documentation textareas: renders [name]{.code}
  as coloured chips when unfocused, raw monospace when focused
- @ inserts markup via autocomplete; select + @ wraps selection
- field stays in edit mode while autocomplete open

SERVER
- refactor/tree 404 fixed: was reading codebook.yaml from SERVE_DIR
  instead of DATA_DIR
- favicon served from assets/resources/favicon.ico

---

### 2026-05-07 — qc-refactor layout overhaul, chip rendering, doc panels, tree diff and corpus impact enhancements

- Three-column layout: op form (left) | staged queue (middle) | preview/script/results (right)
- chipHtml() added — all code references in queue items rendered as coloured left-border chips with ⊕ for stubs
- Foldable doc panel per queue item with full documentation fields using makeRichField(); status locked for deprecate ops
- Tree diff: colour-coded type badges per changed node, inline sibling context for moves/renames, coloured node names
- Corpus impact: full line text with accent highlight, co-occurring codes as chips per excerpt
- Filter exports CODE_COLORS and CODE_SCHEMA; path resolution fixed

---

### 2026-05-08 — qc-refactor op form redesign, doc accordion, column resize

Major overhaul of the op form. Move and Deprecate now use a shared
multi-select picker (buildMultiPickerWithDocs) that fills the available
vertical height. Clicking a code name focuses it for doc editing
independently of the checkbox. Triangle toggle only folds/unfolds
children. Picker search bar full width. Redundant section labels removed
from Move and Deprecate.

Documentation accordion opens below the picker on demand. A resize handle
between the picker and docs redistributes space via CSS grid rows with no
dead space. Full doc fields available for the focused code. Status field
is editable and not locked, allowing codes to be annotated freely during
any operation.

Doc edits auto-queue as a separate docs entry type. buildNameResolutionMap()
resolves renames and merges before writing doc edits at execute time, and
warns if doc edits target a deleted code. Stub form has full inline doc
fields and an editable status selector.

Tree diff now shows colour-coded type badges and inline sibling context.
Corpus impact shows full line text with co-occurring code chips. Column
borders are draggable to resize op / queue / preview panels.

Filter now injects qc-shared.js so isStub, getCodeColor, and codeChip
are available in qc-refactor. CODE_COLORS derived from palette in config.

---

### 2026-05-11 — move parentage fix, reconcile, rename yaml sync, stub detection

Major stability work. Root causes identified and fixed:

**Move parentage** — when a stub was checked and its children
auto-selected, each code got its own move op. _do_move moved the stub
with its block, then each child was moved individually as a sibling. Fix:
filter descendants from move sources at queue time — only the stub is
queued, children come along via block collection.

**Reconciliation** — _reconcile_parents was defined after the class and
never in scope at the call site. Fixed by inlining the logic directly.
Runs after every execute and every docs/save.

**Rename yaml sync** — qc codes rename only updates the corpus. Server
now also renames the node in codebook.yaml after a successful rename.

**Stale name field** — server was writing a name field into every docs
entry on save. Removed. All existing stale name fields purged from
codebook.json.

**Stub detection** — isStub() now checks getDoc().status first, enabling
live detection of status changes without re-rendering.

**Scheme live tree** — scheme polls /refactor/tree every 5s and updates
the sidebar without a full render or page reload.

---

### 2026-05-12 — Session 8: server fixes, rename bug, shared utility audit

#### Server: remove unconditional dirty flag in docs/load and load-json

Migration blocks inside the field normalisation loop set `dirty = True` unconditionally on every iteration regardless of whether any field was missing. Caused `codebook.json` to be rewritten on every page load. Removed the full normalisation block; left only the `_baseline` stripping which is conditional and becomes a no-op once all files are clean. `_reconcile` handles parent sync.

Commit: `fix(server): remove unconditional dirty flag in docs/load and load-json`

#### Server: yaml rename order fix

`qc codes rename` calls `update_codebook()` which writes the yaml, inserting the new code name as a new top-level entry if it is not already present in the yaml at that point. Fix: server-side yaml rename now runs before `qc codes rename`, so the new name is already in the yaml when `update_codebook()` runs and no ghost entry is added.

The yaml rename itself was also changed from a regex sub to a line-by-line exact match: iterates lines, finds the first line whose stripped content exactly equals the old name as a list item (`- name` or `- name:`), replaces it in place preserving indentation.

Commit: `fix(server): yaml rename before qc codes rename to prevent ghost entries`
Commit: `fix(server): yaml rename — line-by-line exact match replaces regex sub`

#### scheme: boot awaits tree refresh before render

`boot()` called `render()` before `refreshTreeFromServer()` resolved, so the initial sidebar was built from the baked `CODEBOOK_TREE` snapshot. Any structural change between the last render and page open showed stale codes until the first 5s poll. Fixed by making `boot()` async and awaiting `refreshTreeFromServer(null)` before `render()`.

Commit: `fix(scheme): await refreshTreeFromServer before first render on boot`

#### scheme: suppress auto-save and changelog write on file open

Every time qc-scheme opened, `clDoc('open', ...)` appended an open event to `state.changelog` and `scheduleSave()` fired, writing `codebook.json` even with no user edits. `clDoc('open')` removed from both the boot-time load and the import load handler. `saveStatus` set to `'saved'` immediately after loading. The changelog now only records structural moves and snapshot saves.

Commit: `fix(scheme): suppress auto-save and changelog write on file open`

#### Tests: move parentage, inline reconcile, rename yaml sync — all confirmed passing

- Move: `Individual_projects` with 3 children queued as one item, executed correctly, yaml nested correctly, reconcile fired.
- Rename: `00_04_Fluff → fluffz → 00_04_Fluff` round-trip confirmed clean after yaml rename order fix. No ghost entries.
- Reconcile: fires after every execute, logs `parent_changes=N ghosts_removed=N`.

#### Shared utility consistency audit

Full audit of all four tools against shared utilities. Findings written into the Deferred/planned section above as a concrete planned session with per-utility action items.

---

## qc-atelier Dev Session 8 — Handoff

**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Transcript:** `/mnt/transcripts/2026-05-13-qc-atelier-dev-session-8.txt` (this session)

---

### Standing architectural rules

These rules are standing constraints for all future development sessions. Claude should apply them without being asked.

1. **Lifecycle logic goes in shared first.** Before adding any function involving data loading, tree state, save state, or server communication to a tool JS file, check whether it belongs in `qc-shared.js`. If it does, add it there and have the tool call it.

2. **Shared CSS is the single source of truth for design tokens.** Tool CSS files add tool-specific rules only. They do not redeclare `:root` variables, body resets, or `.qc-nav` rules that already exist in `qc-shared.css`.

3. **Dark mode:** `body.dark-mode` class (light is default). localStorage key `qca.theme`. All tools use `qcToggleTheme()` from shared.

4. **Navbar HTML:** generated by `M.build_nav(active_tool)` in `qc-shared.lua`. Standard link order: `vz, scheme, refactor, reflect, align, unfold, trace`.

5. **yaml rename runs before `qc codes rename`.** The server must update `codebook.yaml` before calling the qc CLI, so `update_codebook()` finds the new name already present and does not insert a ghost entry.

6. **No file writes on page open.** Loading docs sets `saveStatus = 'saved'`. No changelog event, no `scheduleSave()`.

7. **Unified activity log.** All significant actions across all tools write to `qcLog(tool, type, detail)` in shared. This is the data source for qc-trace. `clDoc` in scheme is a legacy implementation to be replaced.

8. **Reconcile is a safety net, not load-bearing.** `_reconcile` runs after every execute and docs/save. It should find nothing to do in the normal case. If it reports changes on every run, something upstream is creating divergence.

---

### Project context

Single-researcher qualitative coding toolkit. Python 3.14, macOS ARM. Server: `python3 qc-atelier/qc-atelier-server.py` from project root. Public GitHub: CITF-Postdoc. Data in `qc/` submodule on university GitLab.

---

### What was done this session

- Verified move parentage fix (confirmed working)
- Verified rename yaml sync fix (confirmed working after order fix)
- Verified reconcile fires correctly after execute
- Fixed unconditional dirty flag in `docs/load` and `load-json` — codebook.json no longer written on every page load
- Fixed yaml rename order — server renames yaml before calling `qc codes rename`
- Fixed yaml rename method — line-by-line exact match instead of regex
- Fixed scheme boot — awaits tree refresh before first render
- Fixed scheme open event — no auto-save or changelog write on load
- Audited all four tools against shared utilities — findings documented as planned session
- Updated documentation with session log, standing rules, known limitations, and planned session spec

---

### Open issues

- `refactor-queue.json` 404 — harmless, persistent queue never implemented
- `refreshTreeFromServer` re-renders on every poll response with no content-hash check
- Shared utility consistency — planned session documented above
- qc-trace prerequisite: `qcLog` unified activity log not yet implemented
- qc-align, qc-unfold — not started

---

### Key file locations

| File | Path |
|---|---|
| `qc-shared.js` | `qc-atelier/shared/qc-shared.js` |
| `qc-shared.css` | `qc-atelier/shared/qc-shared.css` |
| `qc-shared.lua` | `qc-atelier/shared/qc-shared.lua` |
| `qc-scheme.js` | `qc-atelier/qc-scheme/qc-scheme.js` |
| `qc-scheme-filter.lua` | `qc-atelier/qc-scheme/qc-scheme-filter.lua` |
| `qc-refactor.js` | `qc-atelier/qc-refactor/qc-refactor.js` |
| `qc-refactor-filter.lua` | `qc-atelier/qc-refactor/qc-refactor-filter.lua` |
| `qc-reflect.js` | `qc-atelier/qc-reflect/qc-reflect.js` |
| `qc-reflect-filter.lua` | `qc-atelier/qc-reflect/qc-reflect-filter.lua` |
| `qc-viz.js` | `qc-atelier/qc-viz/qc-viz.js` |
| `qc-viz-filter.lua` | `qc-atelier/qc-viz/qc-viz-filter.lua` |
| `qc-atelier-server.py` | `qc-atelier/qc-atelier-server.py` |
| `codebook.json` | `qc/codebook.json` |
| `codebook.yaml` | `qc/codebook.yaml` |

---

## qc-atelier Dev Session 7 — Handoff

**Date:** 2026-05-11/12
**Transcript:** `/mnt/transcripts/2026-05-11-19-24-44-qc-atelier-dev-session-6.txt` (compacted from session 6; this session has no transcript yet)

---

### Project context

Single-researcher qualitative coding toolkit. Python 3.14, macOS ARM. Server: `python3 qc-atelier/qc-atelier-server.py` from project root. Public GitHub: CITF-Postdoc. Data in `qc/` submodule on university GitLab.

---

### What was done this session

**qc-refactor major overhaul:**
- Three-column layout: op form (left) | staged queue (middle) | preview/script/results (right)
- Move and Deprecate use shared multi-select picker (`buildMultiPickerWithDocs`) with full vertical height, click-to-focus for doc editing, independent checkboxes
- Triangle toggle only folds/unfolds; larger hit area
- Documentation accordion in picker — opens on demand, resizable drag handle between picker and docs
- Column resize handles on panel borders
- Op tabs: Rename / Merge / Move / Deprecate / ⊕ Stub (full names)
- Add to queue pinned at bottom of op column
- Stub form has full inline doc fields and editable status
- Checking a stub auto-checks all descendants; unchecking reverses

**Doc queue system:**
- Doc edits auto-queue as separate `docs` type entries, silent on blur
- `buildNameResolutionMap()` resolves renames/merges before writing docs at execute
- Warns if doc edit targets a code deleted by merge
- `allOps()` excludes doc entries; `doc_entries` sent separately in execute payload

**Queue items:**
- `chipHtml()` renders all code references as coloured left-border chips with ⊕ for stubs

**Tree diff / corpus impact:**
- Colour-coded type badges (renamed/merged/moved/deprecated), inline sibling context
- Full line text with accent highlight, co-occurring codes as chips

**Server fixes:**
- `qc codes rename` no longer enough — server now also renames the node in `codebook.yaml` directly after a successful rename
- Stale `name` field removed from all docs entries and from the save handler
- Move handler filters descendants so only ancestor is moved (children come with block)
- Reconciliation inlined at execute call site — no separate function, syncs `codebook.json` parents to yaml after every execute and every docs/save
- `/codebook/stubs` endpoint added

**qc-scheme:**
- `isStub()` now checks `getDoc(name).status` first (live), then `STUB_CODES`, then name pattern
- All filters export `STUB_CODES` from `status:stub` in codebook.json
- `refreshTreeFromServer()` called on boot and every 5s — tree updates live without re-rendering
- Scroll position preserved around editor swap
- `getChildren`/`getRoots` sort wrapped in try-catch to prevent silent failures

**qc-shared.js:**
- `isStub()` updated with three-tier check
- `makeRichField()` and `parseCodeMarkup()` support `[name]{.code}` markup syntax in all doc fields — renders as coloured left-border chips when unfocused, raw monospace when focused. `@` triggers autocomplete; select text + `@` wraps selection. Available in qc-scheme and qc-refactor doc accordion.

**qc-refactor.js — doc field constants:**
The following constants drive all doc field rendering in the op form accordion and queue doc panels:
```javascript
DOC_FIELDS           // ['scope','rationale','usage_notes','provenance','status']
DOC_FIELD_LABELS     // {scope: 'Scope', rationale: 'Rationale', ...}
DOC_FIELD_HINTS      // {scope: 'What this code captures...', ...}
DOC_FIELD_PLACEHOLDERS // per-field placeholder strings
```
These must remain in sync with `codebook.json` field names.

**Data fixes:**
- `codebook.json`: all stale `name` fields removed, duplicate `10_People` entry removed, parent refs synced
- `codebook.yaml`: `10_People` → `Individuals` fixed; `59_Change` children correctly nested under `Periods`

---

### Open issues

**Move parentage still broken in some cases.** The core bug: when a stub is checked and its children auto-select, each gets its own move op. `_do_move` moves the stub with its block (correct), then each child is moved individually as a sibling (wrong). The JS fix (filter descendants from move sources at queue-add time) was applied this session but **not yet tested** — Zack ran out of patience before verifying.

The yaml after the last execute had `59_Change` at the wrong level — patched manually in outputs. The server-side descendant filter in the move handler was also updated but may still have edge cases.

**Scheme parent mismatch error** — was caused by yaml/json divergence from moves. Should be resolved once reconcile works reliably and the JS descendant filter is confirmed working.

**New parent picker** — opens at 400px, positions upward near viewport bottom, no horizontal overflow. Not explicitly confirmed working after last set of changes.

**Scheme scroll-to-top on row click** — fixed via `window.scrollY` preserve around editor swap in `handleRowClick`. `renderSidebar()` correctly saves and restores `tree.scrollTop` independently. The `_treeScrollTop` variable from outer `render()` scope was removed from `renderSidebar` to avoid undefined reference.

**`refactor-queue.json` 404 in server logs** — harmless. Every refactor page load attempts `GET /docs/load-json?path=.../qc/refactor-queue.json` for a persistent queue feature that was designed but never implemented. Can be ignored.

**`refreshTreeFromServer` polling inefficiency** — scheme polls `/refactor/tree` every 5s and calls `renderSidebar()` on every response regardless of whether the tree changed. No hash/etag check. Acceptable for now but wasteful; a next session could add a content hash comparison before re-rendering.

---

### Key file locations

| File | Path |
|---|---|
| `qc-shared.js` | `qc-atelier/shared/qc-shared.js` |
| `qc-shared.css` | `qc-atelier/shared/qc-shared.css` |
| `qc-scheme.js` | `qc-atelier/qc-scheme/qc-scheme.js` |
| `qc-scheme-filter.lua` | `qc-atelier/qc-scheme/qc-scheme-filter.lua` |
| `qc-refactor.js` | `qc-atelier/qc-refactor/qc-refactor.js` |
| `qc-refactor.css` | `qc-atelier/qc-refactor/qc-refactor.css` |
| `qc-refactor-filter.lua` | `qc-atelier/qc-refactor/qc-refactor-filter.lua` |
| `qc-atelier-server.py` | `qc-atelier/qc-atelier-server.py` |
| `codebook.json` | `qc/codebook.json` |
| `codebook.yaml` | `qc/codebook.yaml` |

---

### Prior conversation titles (chronological)

1. "Qualitative data visualization system" — early qc-viz build
2. "Qualitative coding consistency and deduplication system" — qc-reflect origin
3. "Continuing previous discussion" — qc-viz continuation
4. "qc-viz technical architecture and ecosystem documentation" — March 2026
5. "qc-viz filtering interface not updating after changes" — April 2026
6. "Unifying qualitative coding tools into shared ecosystem" — April 2026
7. "Coding / Workflow help" — fuzzy-concrete GitHub Pages (unrelated)
8. Sessions 1–6 in memory (qc-atelier dev sessions, compacted)
9. **This session** — qc-refactor overhaul, server move/rename fixes, scheme live tree

---

### 2026-05-13 — Shared dark mode, navbar, and CSS token unification

**Dark mode** (`qc-shared.js`): added `qcInitTheme()`, `qcToggleTheme()`,
`qcIsDarkMode()`. Standard across all tools: `body.dark-mode` class (light
is default), `qca.theme` localStorage key, falls back to
`prefers-color-scheme`. Replaces scheme's inline `persistTheme()` /
`LS.set('theme')` and reflect's `body.light` / `qc.theme` implementations.

**Navbar** (`qc-shared.lua`): added `M.build_nav(active)`. Standard link
order: vz, scheme, refactor, reflect, align, unfold, trace. Inactive tools
get `class="inactive"`. All four Lua filters now call `build_nav()` instead
of inlining nav HTML. Reflect had wrong link order and was missing
align/unfold/trace — both fixed.

**CSS** (`qc-scheme.css`, `qc-reflect.css`): removed duplicate `:root` and
`body.dark-mode` blocks from scheme (identical to shared, redundant) and
`:root`, body reset, `body.light`, and `.qc-nav` from reflect (conflicted
with shared). `qc-shared.css` is now the single source of truth for design
tokens, dark mode, reset, and nav styling.

---

### 2026-05-13 — Nav controls: dark mode and snapshot pill across all tools

qc-viz was missing qc-shared.js entirely — dark mode functions were
undefined. Added SHARED_JS_FILE to qc-viz-filter.lua and inlined it
before the tool JS.

nav label "vz" corrected to "viz" in M.build_nav.

reflect.css dark-first token overrides restored as body defaults
(removing :root had broken reflect's appearance since shared.css
is light-first).

Theme toggle button standardised to scheme's style ("☀ Light" /
"☾ Dark") across refactor, viz, reflect. Inline styles removed;
.qc-theme-toggle styled in qc-shared.css.

qcSnapshotPill(containerEl, apiBase) added to qc-shared.js. Fetches
/snapshots/list and shows active_dir stripped of timestamp prefix,
or "HEAD". CSS in qc-shared.css. Appended to the nav bar in
refactor, reflect, and viz — positioned right via margin-left:auto
on the pill, theme toggle follows. Scheme keeps its existing
topbar pill which has richer functionality (↩ HEAD button).

---

```
### 2026-05-14 — Unified nav bar via qcInitNav

**Problem:** scheme had two bars (nav + topbar); refactor, reflect, and viz
had ad-hoc JS appending elements to the nav inconsistently. No shared
structure for the right side of the nav.

**Solution:** `qcInitNav(navEl, opts)` added to `qc-shared.js`. Builds the
right side of the nav identically across all tools:

  separator → stat slot → spacer → snapshot pill → save status → Open → theme toggle

Each slot is optional and driven by opts callbacks. Returns
`{ updateStat, updateSave, updateSnapshot }` for live updates without
full re-renders.

**scheme:** `buildTopbar()` replaced by `buildModeBar()` — mode tabs only,
no stat bar. `renderTopbar()` now calls `_navHandle.update*()` methods.
`qcInitNav` wired in boot with `getStat`, `getSave`, `onOpen`, `onTheme`
callbacks. Old `.topbar`, `.topbar-brand`, `.topbar-sep`, `.topbar-stat`,
`.topbar-space`, `.topbar-theme-toggle` CSS removed from `qc-scheme.css`.

**refactor, reflect, viz:** ad-hoc nav injection blocks replaced with single
`qcInitNav()` call.

**shared.css:** body base added (`background: var(--bg); color: var(--text)`).
Nav height 36px → 42px. Old toggle/pill CSS replaced with slot primitives:
`.qc-nav-sep`, `.qc-nav-stat`, `.qc-nav-space`, `.qc-nav-btn`,
`.qc-nav-save`, `.qc-nav-snapshot`.
```

---

/* qc-reflect.css
   Tokens, reset, body base, dark mode, and nav come from qc-shared.css.
   Reflect uses its own dark colour palette (purple accent vs blue).
   Dark values live under body.dark-mode, not :root, so light mode works. */

/* ── Reflect dark mode token overrides ──────────────────────────────────────
   Applied on top of shared dark-mode when body.dark-mode is set.
   Reflect uses a purple accent rather than shared blue. */
body.dark-mode {
  --bg:         #0f1117;
  --surface:    #1a1d27;
  --surface2:   #222536;
  --border:     #2e3148;
  --border-dim: #232640;
  --text:       #e2e4ef;
  --text-dim:   #7b7f99;
  --text-faint: #444766;
  --accent:     #7c6af7;
  --accent2:    #5b4fd4;
  --green:      #34d399;
  --yellow:     #fbbf24;
  --red:        #f87171;
  --blue:       #60a5fa;
}

/* Reflect layout — full-height flex column */
body {
  height: 100vh;
  overflow: hidden;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}

/* ── Shell ── */
#qc-reflect-root {
  flex: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}

.reflect-shell {
  display: flex;
  flex: 1;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* ── Left column ── */
.reflect-left {
  width: 300px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  border-right: 1px solid var(--border);
  overflow: hidden;
  background: var(--surface);
}

/* ── Left strip ── */
.left-strip {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 6px;
  padding: 5px 10px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.strip-spacer { flex: 1; }

.snapshot-pill { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; }
.snapshot-label {
  font-family: var(--mono);
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  background: var(--surface2);
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: 1px 7px;
}
.snapshot-head-btn {
  background: none;
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: 3px;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 10px;
  padding: 1px 5px;
}
.snapshot-head-btn:hover { color: var(--text-dim); }

.model-badge {
  font-family: var(--mono);
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  white-space: nowrap;
}

.theme-btn {
  background: none;
  border: none;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 12px;
  padding: 2px;
}
.theme-btn:hover { color: var(--text-dim); }

/* ── Suggest buttons ── */
.suggest-btn-row {
  display: flex;
  gap: 6px;
  padding: 8px 10px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.suggest-main-btn { flex: 1; }

/* ── Method legend ── */
.method-legend {
  padding: 6px 10px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 3px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.legend-row {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 6px;
  font-size: 11px;
  color: var(--text-dim);
  cursor: help;
  position: relative;
}
.legend-dot, .method-dot {
  width: 8px;
  height: 8px;
  border-radius: 50%;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.method-co_occurrence  { background: #7c6af7; }
.method-tree_proximity { background: #34d399; }
.method-undercoded     { background: #f87171; }
.method-high_variance  { background: #fbbf24; }
.method-llm_semantic   { background: #60a5fa; }

.legend-label { flex: 1; }
.legend-tip {
  width: 14px;
  height: 14px;
  background: var(--surface2);
  border-radius: 50%;
  text-align: center;
  font-size: 9px;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  line-height: 14px;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.legend-tooltip {
  display: none;
  position: absolute;
  left: 100%;
  top: 0;
  margin-left: 8px;
  background: var(--surface2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
  padding: 6px 10px;
  font-size: 11px;
  color: var(--text-dim);
  width: 220px;
  z-index: 100;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
.legend-row:hover .legend-tooltip { display: block; }

/* ── Suggestion list ── */
.suggest-list {
  flex: 1;
  overflow-y: auto;
  padding: 6px 8px;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 4px;
}

.suggest-progress {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 10px;
  padding: 16px 4px;
}
.suggest-progress-text { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-dim); }
.suggest-error  { color: var(--red);        font-size: 12px; padding: 12px 4px; }
.suggest-empty  { color: var(--text-faint); font-size: 12px; padding: 16px 4px; line-height: 1.8; white-space: pre-line; }

.suggest-card {
  background: var(--bg);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
  padding: 8px 10px;
  cursor: pointer;
  transition: border-color 0.12s, background 0.12s;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 5px;
}
.suggest-card:hover { border-color: var(--border); background: var(--surface2); }
.suggest-card.active { border-color: var(--accent); background: rgba(124,106,247,0.06); }

.suggest-card-head { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; }
.suggest-card-name { flex: 1; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: var(--text); }
.suggest-rank { font-size: 10px; color: var(--text-faint); font-family: var(--mono); flex-shrink: 0; }

.suggest-card-desc { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-dim); line-height: 1.5; }

.suggest-card-codes { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 3px; }
.suggest-code-chip {
  font-family: var(--mono);
  font-size: 10px;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  background: var(--surface2);
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 1px 5px;
  max-width: 120px;
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  white-space: nowrap;
}
.suggest-code-more { font-size: 10px; color: var(--text-faint); padding: 1px 4px; }

.suggest-card-meta { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; }
.suggest-metric {
  font-size: 10px;
  border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 1px 6px;
  font-weight: 600;
}
.method-co_occurrence.suggest-metric  { background: rgba(124,106,247,0.15); color: #7c6af7; }
.method-tree_proximity.suggest-metric { background: rgba(52,211,153,0.15);  color: #34d399; }
.method-undercoded.suggest-metric     { background: rgba(248,113,113,0.15); color: #f87171; }
.method-high_variance.suggest-metric  { background: rgba(251,191,36,0.15);  color: #fbbf24; }
.method-llm_semantic.suggest-metric   { background: rgba(96,165,250,0.15);  color: #60a5fa; }
.suggest-score { font-size: 10px; color: var(--text-faint); font-family: var(--mono); }

/* ── Right column ── */
.reflect-right {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  overflow: hidden;
}

.right-pane {
  flex: 1;
  overflow-y: auto;
  padding: 16px 20px;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 14px;
}

.pane-label {
  font-size: 10px;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  text-transform: uppercase;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.pane-sublabel {
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: var(--text-dim);
  flex-shrink: 0;
  margin-top: 4px;
}

/* ── Corpus overview ── */
.overview-pane { background: var(--bg); }
.overview-stats {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
  gap: 8px;
}
.overview-stat {
  background: var(--surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
  padding: 10px 12px;
  text-align: center;
}
.overview-stat-val { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--accent); font-family: var(--mono); }
.overview-stat-label { font-size: 10px; color: var(--text-faint); margin-top: 2px; }

.treemap-wrap { width: 100%; height: 220px; }
.treemap-wrap svg { display: block; }

.cooc-pairs { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; }
.cooc-pair-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-size: 11px; }
.cooc-pair-codes { width: 240px; font-family: var(--mono); color: var(--text-dim); flex-shrink: 0; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }
.cooc-bar-wrap { flex: 1; height: 8px; background: var(--surface2); border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; }
.cooc-bar { height: 100%; background: var(--accent); border-radius: 4px; opacity: 0.6; }
.cooc-score { font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 10px; color: var(--text-faint); width: 32px; text-align: right; }

/* ── Detail pane ── */
.detail-pane { gap: 12px; }
.detail-header { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap; }
.detail-name { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text); flex: 1; }
.detail-method {
  font-size: 11px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 2px 8px;
  font-weight: 600;
  flex-shrink: 0;
  margin-top: 2px;
}
.detail-desc { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-dim); line-height: 1.7; }
.detail-reason { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-faint); line-height: 1.6; font-style: italic; background: var(--surface); border-left: 2px solid var(--border); padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 0 4px 4px 0; }
.detail-codes { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 4px; }
.detail-code-chip {
  font-family: var(--mono);
  font-size: 11px;
  background: var(--surface2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 2px 8px;
  color: var(--text-dim);
  max-width: 220px;
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

/* ── Layout toggle ── */
.layout-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; flex-shrink: 0; }
.layout-label { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-faint); }
.layout-btn {
  background: var(--surface2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: 4px;
  color: var(--text-faint);
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 11px;
  padding: 3px 10px;
  transition: color 0.1s, border-color 0.1s;
}
.layout-btn:hover { color: var(--text-dim); border-color: var(--border); }
.layout-btn.active { color: var(--accent); border-color: var(--accent); background: rgba(124,106,247,0.1); }

/* ── Network ── */
.network-wrap { width: 100%; height: 280px; background: var(--surface); border-radius: var(--radius); border: 1px solid var(--border-dim); overflow: hidden; flex-shrink: 0; }
.network-wrap svg { display: block; }

/* ── Chart ── */
.chart-wrap { width: 100%; min-height: 160px; background: var(--surface); border-radius: var(--radius); border: 1px solid var(--border-dim); overflow: hidden; flex-shrink: 0; }
.chart-wrap svg { display: block; }

/* ── Run Reflect button ── */
.detail-run-row { flex-shrink: 0; }
.run-reflect-btn {
  width: 100%;
  background: var(--accent);
  border: none;
  border-radius: var(--radius);
  color: #fff;
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 13px;
  font-weight: 600;
  padding: 11px;
  transition: background 0.15s;
}
.run-reflect-btn:hover:not(:disabled) { background: var(--accent2); }
.run-reflect-btn:disabled { background: var(--surface2); color: var(--text-faint); cursor: not-allowed; }

/* ── Run history cards ── */
.run-card {
  background: var(--surface);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
  padding: 10px 12px;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  gap: 5px;
}
.run-card-label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: var(--text); }
.run-card-meta  { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-faint); }
.run-card-error { font-size: 11px; color: var(--red); }
.run-card-btns  { display: flex; gap: 6px; }
.run-card-btn {
  flex: 1;
  background: var(--surface2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: 4px;
  color: var(--text-dim);
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 11px;
  padding: 4px 8px;
  text-align: center;
  text-decoration: none;
  display: block;
  transition: border-color 0.1s;
}
.run-card-btn:hover { border-color: var(--border); }
.run-card-btn.primary { background: rgba(124,106,247,0.1); border-color: var(--accent); color: var(--accent); font-weight: 600; }

/* ── Running pane ── */
.running-pane {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  gap: 12px;
  padding: 40px;
}
.run-log { font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 12px; text-align: center; white-space: pre-wrap; max-width: 400px; }
.run-elapsed { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-faint); font-family: var(--mono); }

/* ── Spinner ── */
.spinner {
  width: 26px; height: 26px;
  border: 2px solid var(--border);
  border-top-color: var(--accent);
  border-radius: 50%;
  animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite;
}
@keyframes spin { to { transform: rotate(360deg); } }

/* ── Shared buttons ── */
.btn {
  background: var(--surface2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border-dim);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
  color: var(--text-dim);
  cursor: pointer;
  font-size: 12px;
  font-family: var(--sans);
  padding: 5px 12px;
  transition: color 0.1s, border-color 0.1s;
}
.btn:hover:not(:disabled) { color: var(--text); border-color: var(--border); }
.btn:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }
.btn.primary { background: rgba(124,106,247,0.12); border-color: var(--accent); color: var(--accent); font-weight: 600; }
.btn.primary:hover:not(:disabled) { background: rgba(124,106,247,0.2); }

/* ── Prompt pane ── */
.prompt-pane { padding: 20px; flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; }
.prompt-text { font-family: var(--mono); font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-dim); white-space: pre-wrap; line-height: 1.7; }

---
